title: Product release notes?

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml:
What’s the best way to know what features or updates have been recently released? I’m aware of this page:

What’s New - monday.com Product Updates

But this doesn’t seem detailed enough. As an example, I use Resource Directory and recently noticed that there is a new field available…Resource manager. Cool! I’ve wanted something like this for months! I wish I could have known this was coming. Or even better, I wish I could have received a notification about this release/update. Instead, I had to stumble across it while working in the tool.

Does Monday.com offer detailed release notes somewhere? The “What’s New” page hits some interesting high notes, but it’s really these small features/updates that excite me and give me incremental goodness between the big features. Thanks!

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @DavidDvorak

monday.com regularly hosts New Features webinars, where they walk through recently released updates/features, highlight smaller enhancements (like fields and column improvements), and share a few practical use cases. You can register for upcoming sessions and also watch past recordings using the link below. These sessions tend to cover many of those “incremental goodness” updates that don’t always get detailed callouts on the What’s New page.
Latest Announcements/Community events topics - monday Community Forum
monday academy | Skills, Training, Courses, Webinars & Certifications

Hope this helps - and glad to hear you discovered the Resource Manager field, it’s a great addition!

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes that’s a good idea, a webinar was how I learned that Sidekick was available and had to be requested to be enabled. Still not quite what I’m looking for. I want to be able to read through release notes and see everything that was introduced recently. I doubt there were any webinars on the “Resource manager” feature I gave as an example. Without this kind of timely transparency customers are delayed in gaining additional value from the tool.
